patterns.description: Force push to main, master, or production branches
patterns.safer_alternative: Use git push --force-with-lease to prevent overwriting others' work, or push to a feature branch instead

patterns.description: git reset --hard on main or master branch
patterns.safer_alternative: Use git revert to create a new commit that undoes changes, preserving history

patterns.description: History rewriting with filter-branch or filter-repo
patterns.safer_alternative: Create a backup branch first (git branch backup-YYYYMMDD), then proceed with rewriting on a separate branch
Use git push --force-with-lease which will fail if the remote has new commits you haven't seen

patterns.description: Interactive or onto rebase that may affect shared branches
patterns.safer_alternative: Create a backup branch before rebasing (git branch backup-YYYYMMDD) and verify the target branch is not shared
Run git clean -n (dry run) first to preview what will be deleted
Only amend unpushed commits. If already pushed, create a new commit instead
Use git stash list to verify the stash entry before dropping
